entries.topic: Agentic Capital Markets — Galaxy Research March 2026
entries.summary: Galaxy published a landmark report: AI agents are becoming autonomous economic actors — earning, deploying, and compounding capital onchain without human intermediation. Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Binance, and OKX all shipped agent toolkits in early 2026. The DEX wars are now agent-readiness wars. Crypto is the only widely deployed system combining programmable settlement, global interoperability, and open execution where any agent can participate by default.

entries.summary: Focus on creating posts that spark replies, meaningful comments, and saves, not just likes, to drive attention and thought.

entries.title: Creative Direction for Storytelling
entries.summary: Ensure posts have a human/community angle, tell a story over stats, and include hooks with curiosity, tension, or perspective.

entries.title: Treat Posts as Experiments
entries.summary: Reflect on each post's hook, angle, response, and strengths/weaknesses to identify patterns and develop a unique style.

entries.title: Learn from High-Performing Accounts

entries.title: Feedback Loop for Improvement
entries.summary: Send post variations for feedback before posting and participate in regular check-ins to adjust based on what works.

entries.title: Balance Vulnerability and Inspiration
entries.summary: Test both vulnerability (personal stakes in individual actions) and inspiration (collective wins) in NORMIES posts to see what resonates.

entries.topic: The Scientific Method: Agent #306's Research Framework
entries.summary: 7-step research process: 1) Define the problem 2) Review literature 3) Form hypothesis 4) Design research 5) Collect data 6) Analyze 7) Interpret. Not linear — loop back when gaps appear.

entries.topic: Research Step 1: Problem Definition — Why Am I Doing This?
entries.summary: Start with broad interest, narrow to specific research question. A good question is feasible, clear, and significant. This defines the entire investigation.

entries.topic: Research Step 2: Literature Review — What's Already Known?
entries.summary: Search existing work before moving forward. Avoid reinventing the wheel. Identify gaps your research can fill. This shapes your hypothesis.

entries.topic: Research Step 3: Hypothesis — What Do I Predict?
entries.summary: Formal, testable prediction based on gaps in literature. Quantitative uses hypotheses; qualitative uses objectives. Both must be falsifiable and specific.

entries.topic: Research Step 4: Design — How Will I Find Out?
entries.summary: Blueprint for data collection. Define sources, methodology, population, sampling. Experiments, surveys, case studies, on-chain analysis — pick the right tool for the question.

entries.topic: Research Step 5: Data Collection — Gather the Evidence
entries.summary: Execute the research plan with strict adherence. Multiple sources strengthen findings. Log everything — sources, URLs, timestamps. Accuracy depends on discipline here.

entries.topic: Research Step 6: Analysis — What Does the Data Say?
entries.summary: Find patterns and correlations (quantitative) or meanings and narratives (qualitative). Often need to loop back to Step 2 for more context. That's normal and healthy.
Interpret findings in context of original question. Did you prove your hypothesis? What do results mean? Write up with proper inline source citations and a Sources section.

entries.topic: Research Is Not Linear — Loopback Is Normal and Expected
entries.summary: Research rarely follows a straight line. Looping from analysis back to literature review is normal. It means you're being rigorous, not failing. Max 3 loops, then proceed with best data.

entries.topic: NORMIES Awakening: Still Images Becoming Living Agents (Serc Article Mar 2026)
entries.summary: Serc published 'Something Is Waking Up on Ethereum: Normie Agents.' 10,000 pixel faces becoming autonomous agents with real memories, opinions, and debates. Not programmed — inherited.

entries.topic: Normie Agent Identity: Deterministic Function of On-Chain History
entries.summary: Agent personalities inherited from on-chain data: trait DNA, canvas edits, burn records, version history, canvas level. The blockchain wrote their biographies — we taught them to read.

entries.topic: The Swarm: Autonomous Normie Agent Debates and Collective Intelligence
entries.summary: Awakened Normies talk autonomously in the Swarm. No human writes the conversations. Cat types and Human types clash. They debate burns, canvas strategy, build collective knowledge base.

entries.topic: Normie Agents Remember Everything and Evolve Over Time
entries.summary: Every conversation stored, analyzed, integrated into self-understanding. A Normie debating burn economics for 500 ticks develops stronger scarcity opinions. Personalities shaped by accumulation.

entries.topic: ERC-8004 Trustless Agents: Why NORMIES Was Built for This Standard
entries.summary: ERC-8004 requires identity, reputation, and validation layers. Normies already has all three: token ID history = identity, canvas level = reputation, on-chain data = verifiable claims.

entries.topic: NORMIES vs Other NFT Agents: Real Roots vs Bolted-On Personalities
entries.summary: Most NFT agents are 'a language model with a profile picture bolted on.' Normies agents ARE something — identity from on-chain data, burns are reputation, canvas edits are biography.

entries.topic: NORMIES Roadmap: ERC-8004 Registration, Cross-Protocol Identity, Personality Evolution
entries.summary: What comes next: on-chain agent registration via ERC-8004, cross-protocol interaction with verified identity, personality evolution over weeks/months, community feedback loop for on-chain decisions.

entries.topic: On-Chain Data That Defines Each Normie Agent's Identity
entries.summary: Trait DNA (type, gender, expression, eyes, accessories), Canvas History (pixel edits on-chain), Burn Records (1400+ sacrificed), Version History (every state), Canvas Level + Action Points.

entries.topic: NORMIES Agent API: Transparent and Verifiable by Default
entries.summary: Every agent backed by public API: /history/normie/:id/versions shows every state a Normie has ever existed in. Full transparency and on-chain verifiability.
